title: SC2 Agent Opponent Priors
description: Per-(opponent, matchup) memory the bot writes to ./data/opponents/<opponent_name>_<matchup>.json after each match and reads at game start. This is the in-match adaptation tier: lets the bot bias playbook execution based on what we've seen this opponent do before, without needing the offline strategist to re-run. Distinct from the playbook itself, which is matchup-wide.

opponent_name.description: Opponent bot's name as reported by aiarena. Filename is <opponent_name>_<matchup>.json (sanitized).

matchup.description: Matchup these priors apply to. An opponent that plays Random gets one file per race they roll.

observations.description: Append-only log of recent matches against this opponent. Trimmed to most-recent N (default 50) when written, to keep file size bounded.

derived.description: Summary computed from observations after every match write. The bot reads ONLY this section at game start; observations are kept for offline analysis by the strategist.

confidence.description: How much weight to give these priors. Increases with match_count, decreases with variance across observations. Below ~0.3 the bot should mostly ignore these and rely on the playbook.

build_signature.description: Tag for the opponent's typical opening (e.g. 'speedling_allin', 'fast_third_into_roach', 'muta_tech', '12_pool', 'cannon_rush'). Vocabulary defined by the bot — unknown values must be ignored on read with a warning.

expected_first_attack_seconds.description: Median in-game time of opponent's first significant attack across recorded observations. The bot uses this to advance defensive prep.

expected_expansion_seconds.description: Median in-game time opponent took natural expansion. Null if they reliably skip it (all-in tendencies).

prepop_reactions.description: Reaction trigger names to activate at game start as if already scouted. Bot enforces vocabulary; unknown names ignored with warning. Used to pre-load defensive responses for highly predictable opponents.

expected_composition.description: Mid-game composition observed in past matches. Same shape as composition_map in the playbook schema. Bot uses this to bias tech targeting.

loss_patterns.description: Short free-form tags describing recurring causes of our losses vs this opponent. Mainly a signal for the offline strategist's postmortem; the bot does not parse these.

our_playbook_version.description: Schema_version + generator tag from the playbook we used. Lets the strategist segregate observations by which playbook generated them.

their_opening.description: First key timings observed via scouting. Race-agnostic.

key_buildings_seen.description: Map of python-sc2 UnitTypeId -> supply at which we first scouted that structure. Examples: {SupplyDepot: 14, Barracks: 16}, {SpawningPool: 17, Extractor: 19}, {Pylon: 14, Gateway: 16}.

first_tech_structure.description: PascalCase python-sc2 UnitTypeId of the first key tech structure we saw (e.g. 'RoachWarren', 'BanelingNest', 'BarracksTechLab', 'TwilightCouncil').

composition.description: Unit-type -> count snapshot at first significant engagement.

our_scouted_reactions_fired.description: Reaction trigger names that fired during this match.

our_critical_event.description: Short tag for the pivotal moment that decided the match. Vocabulary defined by the bot (e.g. 'lost_natural_to_roach_baneling_at_5_00', 'killed_their_third_at_7_30', 'attrition_macro_loss').

notes.description: Optional free-form note. Mainly useful when the strategist or a human writes back to a file during analysis.
